sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Allergic Rhinitis
sources.population: Adults and children presenting with allergic-rhinitis complaints; age-specific medicine details are not mapped.
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for Dubai telehealth allergic-rhinitis documentation; partial for broad nasal obstruction and non-allergic causes.

exact_sections.heading: 5.1 Virtual Clinical Assessment; 6. Clinical History/Symptoms
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists sneezing, itching, rhinorrhoea, postnasal drip, congestion, smell change, eye symptoms, timing, and allergen or occupational triggers.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Red Flags
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists unilateral symptoms, visual change, epistaxis, fever, facial pain, purulent or bloody discharge, and systemic concerns.

exact_sections.heading: 9. Investigations
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Describes clinician-selected specific-IgE testing by skin-prick or serum when confirmation is relevant.

exact_sections.heading: 10. Management; 12. Referral Criteria
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led allergen education, management options, structural obstruction, non-response, imaging decisions, and referral criteria.
